content.claim: Hungary has the highest level of segregation of students from less and more advantaged socioeconomic groups into different schools in the EU, with the most and least disadvantaged students showing the highest segregation levels in the 2022 PISA study.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Correlates segregation with school system features but does not establish causal chain to 6/8-year tracks specifically
Structural factors such as residential segregation and longstanding admission policies identified as likely primary drivers rather than track structure alone
Does not measure counterfactual: what segregation would be under alternative secondary school structures
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
